1: package authtoken
2: 
3: import (
4: 	"context"
5: 	"fmt"
6: 	"time"
7: 
8: 	"github.com/golang/protobuf/ptypes"

10: 	"github.com/hashicorp/boundary/internal/db/timestamp"
11: 	"github.com/hashicorp/boundary/internal/errors"
12: 	"github.com/hashicorp/boundary/internal/iam"
13: 	"github.com/hashicorp/boundary/internal/kms"
14: )
15: 
16: var (
17: 	lastAccessedUpdateDuration = 10 * time.Minute
18: 	timeSkew                   = time.Duration(0)
19: )
20: 
21: // A Repository stores and retrieves the persistent types in the authtoken
22: // package. It is not safe to use a repository concurrently.
23: type Repository struct {
24: 	reader              db.Reader
25: 	writer              db.Writer
26: 	kms                 *kms.Kms
27: 	limit               int
28: 	timeToLiveDuration  time.Duration
29: 	timeToStaleDuration time.Duration
30: }
31: 
32: // NewRepository creates a new Repository. The returned repository is not safe for concurrent go
33: // routines to access it.
34: func NewRepository(r db.Reader, w db.Writer, kms *kms.Kms, opt ...Option) (*Repository, error) {
35: 	const op = "authtoken.NewRepository"
36: 	switch {
37: 	case r == nil:
38: 		return nil, errors.NewDeprecated(errors.InvalidParameter, op, "nil db reader")
39: 	case w == nil:
40: 		return nil, errors.NewDeprecated(errors.InvalidParameter, op, "nil db writer")
41: 	case kms == nil:
42: 		return nil, errors.NewDeprecated(errors.InvalidParameter, op, "nil kms")
43: 	}
44: 
45: 	opts := getOpts(opt...)
46: 
47: 	return &Repository{
48: 		reader:              r,
49: 		writer:              w,
50: 		kms:                 kms,
51: 		limit:               opts.withLimit,
52: 		timeToLiveDuration:  opts.withTokenTimeToLiveDuration,
53: 		timeToStaleDuration: opts.withTokenTimeToStaleDuration,
54: 	}, nil
55: }
56: 
57: // CreateAuthToken inserts an Auth Token into the repository and returns a new
58: // Auth Token.  The returned auth token contains the auth token value. The
59: // provided IAM User ID must be associated to the provided auth account id or an
60: // error will be returned.  The Auth Token will have a Status of "issued".
61: // The WithStatus and WithPublicId options are supported and all other options
62: // are ignored.
63: func (r *Repository) CreateAuthToken(ctx context.Context, withIamUser *iam.User, withAuthAccountId string, opt ...Option) (*AuthToken, error) {
64: 	const op = "authtoken.(Repository).CreateAuthToken"
65: 	if withIamUser == nil || withIamUser.User == nil {
66: 		return nil, errors.New(ctx, errors.InvalidParameter, op, "missing user")
67: 	}
68: 	if withIamUser.GetPublicId() == "" {
69: 		return nil, errors.New(ctx, errors.InvalidParameter, op, "missing user id")
70: 	}
71: 	if withAuthAccountId == "" {
72: 		return nil, errors.New(ctx, errors.InvalidParameter, op, "missing auth account id")
73: 	}
74: 	at, err := newAuthToken()
75: 	if err != nil {
76: 		return nil, errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op)
77: 	}
78: 	at.AuthAccountId = withAuthAccountId
79: 	opts := getOpts(opt...)
80: 	if opts.withPublicId == "" {
81: 		id, err := NewAuthTokenId()
82: 		if err != nil {
83: 			return nil, errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op)
84: 		}
85: 		opts.withPublicId = id
86: 	}
87: 	at.PublicId = opts.withPublicId
88: 
89: 	switch {
90: 	case opts.withStatus != "":
91: 		at.Status = string(PendingStatus)
92: 	default:
93: 		at.Status = string(IssuedStatus)
94: 	}
95: 
96: 	databaseWrapper, err := r.kms.GetWrapper(ctx, withIamUser.GetScopeId(), kms.KeyPurposeDatabase)
97: 	if err != nil {
98: 		return nil, errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op, errors.WithMsg("unable to get database wrapper"))
99: 	}
100: 
101: 	// We truncate the expiration time to the nearest second to make testing in different platforms with
102: 	// different time resolutions easier.
103: 	expiration, err := ptypes.TimestampProto(time.Now().Add(r.timeToLiveDuration).Truncate(time.Second))
104: 	if err != nil {
105: 		return nil, errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op, errors.WithCode(errors.InvalidTimeStamp))
106: 	}
107: 	at.ExpirationTime = &timestamp.Timestamp{Timestamp: expiration}
108: 
109: 	var newAuthToken *AuthToken
110: 	_, err = r.writer.DoTx(
111: 		ctx,
112: 		db.StdRetryCnt,
113: 		db.ExpBackoff{},
114: 		func(read db.Reader, w db.Writer) error {
115: 			acct := allocAuthAccount()
116: 			acct.PublicId = withAuthAccountId
117: 			if err := read.LookupByPublicId(ctx, acct); err != nil {
118: 				return errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op, errors.WithMsg("auth account lookup"))
119: 			}
120: 			if acct.GetIamUserId() != withIamUser.GetPublicId() {
121: 				return errors.New(ctx, errors.InvalidParameter, op,
122: 					fmt.Sprintf("auth account %q mismatch with iam user %q", withAuthAccountId, withIamUser.GetPublicId()))
123: 			}
124: 			at.ScopeId = acct.GetScopeId()
125: 			at.AuthMethodId = acct.GetAuthMethodId()
126: 			at.IamUserId = acct.GetIamUserId()
127: 
128: 			newAuthToken = at.clone()
129: 			if err := newAuthToken.encrypt(ctx, databaseWrapper); err != nil {
130: 				return errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op)
131: 			}
132: 			// tokens are not replicated, so they don't need oplog entries.
133: 			if err := w.Create(ctx, newAuthToken); err != nil {
134: 				return errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op)
135: 			}
136: 			newAuthToken.CtToken = nil
137: 
138: 			return nil
139: 		},
140: 	)
141: 
142: 	if err != nil {
143: 		return nil, errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op)
144: 	}
145: 	return newAuthToken, nil
146: }
147: 
148: // LookupAuthToken returns the AuthToken for the provided id. Returns nil, nil if no AuthToken is found for id.
149: // For security reasons, the actual token is not included in the returned AuthToken.
150: // All exported options are ignored.
151: func (r *Repository) LookupAuthToken(ctx context.Context, id string, opt ...Option) (*AuthToken, error) {
152: 	const op = "authtoken.(Repository).LookupAuthToken"
153: 	if id == "" {
154: 		return nil, errors.New(ctx, errors.InvalidPublicId, op, "missing public id")
155: 	}
156: 	opts := getOpts(opt...)
157: 
158: 	// use the view, to bring in the required account columns. Just don't forget
159: 	// to convert it before returning it.
160: 	atv := allocAuthTokenView()
161: 	atv.PublicId = id
162: 	if err := r.reader.LookupByPublicId(ctx, atv); err != nil {
163: 		if errors.IsNotFoundError(err) {
164: 			return nil, nil
165: 		}
166: 		return nil, errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op)
167: 	}
168: 
169: 	at := atv.toAuthToken()
170: 	if opts.withTokenValue {
171: 		databaseWrapper, err := r.kms.GetWrapper(ctx, at.GetScopeId(), kms.KeyPurposeDatabase, kms.WithKeyId(at.GetKeyId()))
172: 		if err != nil {
173: 			return nil, errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op, errors.WithCode(errors.Encrypt), errors.WithMsg("unable to get database wrapper"))
174: 		}
175: 		if err := at.decrypt(ctx, databaseWrapper); err != nil {
176: 			return nil, errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op)
177: 		}
178: 	}
179: 
180: 	at.CtToken = nil
181: 	at.KeyId = ""
182: 	return at, nil
183: }
184: 
185: // ValidateToken returns a token from storage if the auth token with the provided id and token exists.  The
186: // approximate last accessed time may be updated depending on how long it has been since the last time the token
187: // was validated.  If a token is returned it is guaranteed to be valid. For security reasons, the actual token
188: // value is not included in the returned AuthToken. If no valid auth token is found nil, nil is returned.
189: // All options are ignored.
190: //
191: // NOTE: Do not log or add the token string to any errors to avoid leaking it as it is a secret.
192: func (r *Repository) ValidateToken(ctx context.Context, id, token string, opt ...Option) (*AuthToken, error) {
193: 	const op = "authtoken.(Repository).ValidateToken"
194: 	if token == "" {
195: 		return nil, errors.New(ctx, errors.InvalidParameter, op, "missing token")
196: 	}
197: 	if id == "" {
198: 		return nil, errors.New(ctx, errors.InvalidPublicId, op, "missing public id")
199: 	}
200: 
201: 	retAT, err := r.LookupAuthToken(ctx, id, withTokenValue())
202: 	if err != nil {
203: 		retAT = nil
204: 		if errors.IsNotFoundError(err) {
205: 			return nil, nil
206: 		}
207: 		return nil, errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op)
208: 	}
209: 	if retAT == nil {
210: 		return nil, nil
211: 	}
212: 
213: 	// If the token is too old or stale invalidate it and return nothing.
214: 	exp, err := ptypes.Timestamp(retAT.GetExpirationTime().GetTimestamp())
215: 	if err != nil {
216: 		return nil, errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op, errors.WithMsg("expiration time"), errors.WithCode(errors.InvalidTimeStamp))
217: 	}
218: 	lastAccessed, err := ptypes.Timestamp(retAT.GetApproximateLastAccessTime().GetTimestamp())
219: 	if err != nil {
220: 		return nil, errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op, errors.WithMsg("last accessed time"), errors.WithCode(errors.InvalidTimeStamp))
221: 	}
222: 
223: 	now := time.Now()
224: 	sinceLastAccessed := now.Sub(lastAccessed) + timeSkew
225: 	// TODO (jimlambrt 9/2020) - investigate the need for the timeSkew and see
226: 	// if it can be eliminated.
227: 	if now.After(exp.Add(-timeSkew)) || sinceLastAccessed >= r.timeToStaleDuration {
228: 		// If the token has expired or has become too stale, delete it from the DB.
229: 		_, err = r.writer.DoTx(
230: 			ctx,
231: 			db.StdRetryCnt,
232: 			db.ExpBackoff{},
233: 			func(_ db.Reader, w db.Writer) error {
234: 				delAt := retAT.clone()
235: 				// tokens are not replicated, so they don't need oplog entries.
236: 				if _, err := w.Delete(ctx, delAt); err != nil {
237: 					return errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op, errors.WithMsg("delete auth token"))
238: 				}
239: 				retAT = nil
240: 				return nil
241: 			})
242: 		if err != nil {
243: 			return nil, errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op)
244: 		}
245: 		return nil, nil
246: 	}
247: 
248: 	if retAT.GetToken() != token {
249: 		return nil, nil
250: 	}
251: 	// retAT.Token set to empty string so the value is not returned as described in the methods' doc.
252: 	retAT.Token = ""
253: 
254: 	if sinceLastAccessed >= lastAccessedUpdateDuration {
255: 		// To save the db from being updated too frequently, we only update the
256: 		// LastAccessTime if it hasn't been updated within lastAccessedUpdateDuration.
257: 		// TODO: Make this duration configurable.
258: 		_, err = r.writer.DoTx(
259: 			ctx,
260: 			db.StdRetryCnt,
261: 			db.ExpBackoff{},
262: 			func(_ db.Reader, w db.Writer) error {
263: 				at := retAT.clone()
264: 				// Setting the ApproximateLastAccessTime to null through using the null mask allows a defined db's
265: 				// trigger to set ApproximateLastAccessTime to the commit
266: 				// timestamp. Tokens are not replicated, so they don't need oplog entries.
267: 				rowsUpdated, err := w.Update(
268: 					ctx,
269: 					at,
270: 					nil,
271: 					[]string{"ApproximateLastAccessTime"},
272: 				)
273: 				if err != nil {
274: 					return errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op)
275: 				}
276: 				if rowsUpdated > 1 {
277: 					return errors.New(ctx, errors.MultipleRecords, op, "more than 1 resource would have been updated")
278: 				}
279: 				return nil
280: 			},
281: 		)
282: 	}
283: 
284: 	if err != nil {
285: 		return nil, errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op, errors.WithMsg(id))
286: 	}
287: 	return retAT, nil
288: }

290: // ListAuthTokens lists auth tokens in the given scopes and supports the
291: // WithLimit option.
292: func (r *Repository) ListAuthTokens(ctx context.Context, withScopeIds []string, opt ...Option) ([]*AuthToken, error) {
293: 	const op = "authtoken.(Repository).ListAuthTokens"
294: 	if len(withScopeIds) == 0 {
295: 		return nil, errors.New(ctx, errors.InvalidParameter, op, "missing scope id")
296: 	}
297: 	opts := getOpts(opt...)
298: 
299: 	// use the view, to bring in the required account columns. Just don't forget
300: 	// to convert them before returning them
301: 	var atvs []*authTokenView
302: 	if err := r.reader.SearchWhere(ctx, &atvs, "auth_account_id in (select public_id from auth_account where scope_id in (?))", []interface{}{withScopeIds}, db.WithLimit(opts.withLimit)); err != nil {
303: 		return nil, errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op)
304: 	}
305: 	authTokens := make([]*AuthToken, 0, len(atvs))
306: 	for _, atv := range atvs {
307: 		atv.Token = ""
308: 		atv.CtToken = nil
309: 		atv.KeyId = ""
310: 		authTokens = append(authTokens, atv.toAuthToken())
311: 	}
312: 	return authTokens, nil
313: }
314: 
315: // DeleteAuthToken deletes the token with the provided id from the repository returning a count of the
316: // number of records deleted.  All options are ignored.
317: func (r *Repository) DeleteAuthToken(ctx context.Context, id string, opt ...Option) (int, error) {
318: 	const op = "authtoken.(Repository).DeleteAuthToken"
319: 	if id == "" {
320: 		return db.NoRowsAffected, errors.New(ctx, errors.InvalidPublicId, op, "missing public id")
321: 	}
322: 
323: 	at, err := r.LookupAuthToken(ctx, id)
324: 	if err != nil {
325: 		if errors.IsNotFoundError(err) {
326: 			return db.NoRowsAffected, nil
327: 		}
328: 		return db.NoRowsAffected, errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op)
329: 	}
330: 	if at == nil {
331: 		return db.NoRowsAffected, nil
332: 	}
333: 
334: 	var rowsDeleted int
335: 	_, err = r.writer.DoTx(
336: 		ctx,
337: 		db.StdRetryCnt,
338: 		db.ExpBackoff{},
339: 		func(_ db.Reader, w db.Writer) error {
340: 			deleteAT := at.clone()
341: 			// tokens are not replicated, so they don't need oplog entries.
342: 			rowsDeleted, err = w.Delete(ctx, deleteAT)
343: 			if err != nil {
344: 				return errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op)
345: 			}
346: 			if rowsDeleted > 1 {
347: 				return errors.New(ctx, errors.MultipleRecords, op, "more than 1 resource would have been deleted")
348: 			}
349: 			return nil
350: 		},
351: 	)
352: 
353: 	if err != nil {
354: 		return db.NoRowsAffected, errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op, errors.WithMsg(id))
355: 	}
356: 
357: 	return rowsDeleted, nil
358: }
359: 
360: // IssueAuthToken will retrieve the "pending" token and update it's status to
361: // "issued".  If the token has already been issued, an error is returned with a
362: // nil token.  If no token is found for the tokenRequestId an error is returned
363: // with a nil token.
364: //
365: // Note: no oplog entries are created for auth token operations (this is intentional).
366: func (r *Repository) IssueAuthToken(ctx context.Context, tokenRequestId string) (*AuthToken, error) {
367: 	const op = "authtoken.(Repository).IssueAuthToken"
368: 	if tokenRequestId == "" {
369: 		return nil, errors.New(ctx, errors.InvalidParameter, op, "missing token request id")
370: 	}
371: 
372: 	var at *AuthToken
373: 	_, err := r.writer.DoTx(
374: 		ctx,
375: 		db.StdRetryCnt,
376: 		db.ExpBackoff{},
377: 		func(reader db.Reader, w db.Writer) error {
378: 			at = allocAuthToken()
379: 			at.PublicId = tokenRequestId
380: 			at.Status = string(IssuedStatus)
381: 			// note: no oplog operations are created for auth token operations (this is intentional).
382: 			// Setting the ApproximateLastAccessTime to null through using the null mask allows a defined db's
383: 			// trigger to set ApproximateLastAccessTime to the commit timestamp.
384: 			rowsUpdated, err := w.Update(ctx, at, []string{"Status"}, []string{"ApproximateLastAccessTime"}, db.WithWhere("status = ?", PendingStatus))
385: 			if err != nil {
386: 				return errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op, errors.WithoutEvent())
387: 			}
388: 			if rowsUpdated == 0 {
389: 				return errors.New(ctx, errors.RecordNotFound, op, "pending auth token not found")
390: 			}
391: 			if rowsUpdated > 1 {
392: 				return errors.New(ctx, errors.Internal, op, fmt.Sprintf("should have updated 1 row and we attempted to update %d rows", rowsUpdated))
393: 			}
394: 			if at.Status != string(IssuedStatus) {
395: 				return errors.New(ctx, errors.Internal, op, "updated auth token status != issued")
396: 			}
397: 
398: 			// we need a new repo, that's using the same reader/writer as this TxHandler
399: 			txRepo := Repository{
400: 				reader: reader,
401: 				writer: w,
402: 				kms:    r.kms,
403: 			}
404: 			at, err = txRepo.LookupAuthToken(ctx, at.PublicId, withTokenValue())
405: 			if err != nil {
406: 				return errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op)
407: 			}
408: 			if at == nil {
409: 				return errors.New(ctx, errors.RecordNotFound, op, "issued auth token not found")
410: 			}
411: 			return nil
412: 		})
413: 	if err != nil {
414: 		return nil, err // error already wrapped when raised from r.DoTx(...)
415: 	}
416: 	return at, nil
417: }
418: 
419: // CloseExpiredPendingTokens will close expired pending tokens in the repo.
420: // This function should called on a periodic basis a Controllers via it's
421: // "ticker" pattern.
422: func (r *Repository) CloseExpiredPendingTokens(ctx context.Context) (int, error) {
423: 	const op = "authtoken.(Repository).CloseExpiredPendingTokens"
424: 
425: 	args := []interface{}{string(FailedStatus), string(PendingStatus)}
426: 	const sql = `update auth_token set status = ? where status = ? and now() > expiration_time`
427: 	var tokensClosed int
428: 	_, err := r.writer.DoTx(
429: 		ctx,
430: 		db.StdRetryCnt,
431: 		db.ExpBackoff{},
432: 		func(_ db.Reader, w db.Writer) error {
433: 			var err error
434: 			tokensClosed, err = w.Exec(ctx, sql, args)
435: 			if err != nil {
436: 				return errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op)
437: 			}
438: 			return nil
439: 		},
440: 	)
441: 	if err != nil {
442: 		return db.NoRowsAffected, err // error already wrapped when raised from r.DoTx(...)
443: 	}
444: 	return tokensClosed, nil
445: }
